subject: Project Management
title: The Empire State Building
description: A micro-lesson that makes The Empire State Building usable.

hook.text: The Empire State Building was built in just 410 days. That's 102 floors in barely a year.

buildup.text: At peak construction, 3,400 workers added four and a half floors per week.

discovery.text: A dedicated rail line delivered materials to the site. Lunch carts served food on steel beams above the city.

twist.text: It opened during the Great Depression and sat half-empty for years. Critics called it 'Empty State Building.'

climax.text: The building that was a financial failure became New York's most beloved icon.

punchline.text: Built in a year. Loved for a century.

quiz.question: How long did it take to build the Empire State Building?
